description_long: This is a vintage, single-propeller biplane with a streamlined fuselage painted in a muted greenish-gray color, accented by light tan wings and tail surfaces. The wings are straight and slightly angled, featuring roundels and bracing wires for structural support. The engine is mounted at the nose, exposed with visible cylinders and a large wooden propeller. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the engine, likely accommodating a single pilot. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a tail skid, all fixed and non-retractable, giving the aircraft a classic early 20th-century appearance.
description: A vintage single-propeller biplane with tan wings, greenish-gray fuselage, open cockpit, and fixed landing gear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the exposed engine, open cockpit, and the full length of the straight tan wings with roundels.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the wingspan, bracing wires, and symmetrical placement of roundels on both wings.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the propeller, engine details, and the tail's vertical stabilizer.
description_view_3: Frontal view focusing on the wooden propeller, engine, and the fixed main landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing monoplane with straight wings, slight dihedral angle, and no winglets or sweep.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with exposed cylinders and a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit located just behind the engine.
